current_suggestion.pitch: Your current channels give you Peter Attia's longevity framing and Michael Saylor's Bitcoin maximalism, but nobody in your lineup steelmans a principled anti-state philosophical framework — Dave Smith combines Stoic-adjacent personal responsibility ethics with Austrian economics and Bitcoin monetary theory in a way that would pressure-test assumptions you're forming elsewhere. He covers monetary policy, individual sovereignty, and epistemics around consensus narratives (including health and media) from a libertarian philosophy angle that's distinct from Rogan's broader eclecticism. Format is a solo podcast monologue plus occasional long-form interviews, consistently analytical rather than sensationalist.

history: TEDx Talks – Russ Roberts (EconTalk host solo appearances)
EP Wealth Advisors (with Ben Felix-style rigor — try 'The Plain Bagel' instead)
Odyssey of the Mind (William MacAskill / 80,000 Hours)
